name: Tomatoes and Yucca Root Relish on Snapper
recipeIngredient: 1 cup peeled yucca root, finely chopped
1 1/2 pounds red snapper fillets, thawed if frozen
2 tablespoons butter or margarine, melted, optional
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). In a steamer basket over simmering water, cook yucca root, onion and carrots for 8 to 10 minutes or until nearly tender. Add zucchini; steam for an additional 2 minutes. Toss the cooked vegetables with tomatoes, thyme, bay leaf, salt, and cayenne pepper in a medium bowl. Arrange fish fillets in a lightly oiled shallow baking dish. Cover and bake for 15 minutes. Spoon the vegetable mixture over the fillets; return to oven. Bake an additional 10 to 15 minutes or until the fish flakes easily when tested with a fork. Drizzle with butter or margarine. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Vegetable Topped Snapper: Any firm whitefish can substitute for the red snapper. Yucca root is also called cassava and must be peeled and cooked before eating (International Produce Cookbook and Guide, HPBooks 1989, Marlene Brown). YUKK-ah from Brazil; also "yuca." It has a very large, elongated root with thick brown skin that is often waxed to hold in moisture. The flesh is white and has a mild, potato-like flavor. It contains about 100 calories per 3.5 oz serving and is rich in Vit A and Thiamin. Do not eat raw; cook like a potato.
